hero.subtitle: Power BI • SQL • Python • ETL Automation • Reporting Systems
hero.description: I help startups and enterprises replace spreadsheet chaos with automated Power BI dashboards and reliable data pipelines. Over 4+ years, delivered 200+ enterprise reporting solutions across media intelligence and financial services — cutting manual reporting time by 45% with 99.5% data accuracy. Direct engagement, transparent pricing, European & global clients.

paragraphs: I am an independent Data Analyst and BI Consultant with 4+ years of experience building automated reporting solutions for enterprise clients across media intelligence and financial services — 200+ engagements delivered.

I specialize in end-to-end reporting automation — from SQL query optimisation and ETL pipeline design to interactive Power BI dashboards and executive-ready narratives. Track record: reducing manual reporting effort by 45%, enforcing 99.5% data accuracy, and mentoring analysts to scale team output.
I work directly with businesses that need reliable, self-serve reporting — without the overhead of a full-time BI team. Currently serving clients across Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific.
Open to project-based, retainer, and consulting engagements — from one-time dashboard builds to ongoing analytics partnerships and Power BI training programmes.

skills.sectionDesc: Professional competencies built over 4+ years of enterprise data analytics and BI automation.

experience.title: Freelance Data Analyst
bullets: Deliver end-to-end analytics solutions for European and global clients needing automated reporting, data pipeline cleanup, and BI dashboard development.
Automate reporting workflows using SQL + Power BI, reducing manual effort by 30% and improving turnaround time for recurring deliverables.
Troubleshoot data quality issues across multi-source datasets, ensuring minimal disruption to client delivery timelines across different time zones.
Produce data-driven reports with KPI charts (Pie, Bar, Column) and robust qualitative insights using established reporting templates.
Manage concurrent deliverables for multiple international clients via Freelancer.com platform with 5-star ratings and repeat business.

experience.title: Data Analyst
bullets: Generated and managed data foundations that drove media intelligence and financial reports for 200+ enterprise clients, ensuring accurate and timely deliverables.
Ensured coding analysis production consistency across multi-wave research datasets; tracked performance against strict timelines and maintained 99.5% data accuracy.
Mentored junior coding analysts, delivering feedback on best practices, addressing performance gaps, and fostering continuous skill development.
Developed optimized SQL queries (joins, subqueries, performance tuning) for large-scale data extraction, validation, and consistency checks.
Reduced manual reporting effort by 45% and improved turnaround by 35% through dashboard automation, SQL pipeline optimization, and reporting tracker automation.

experience.title: Data Analyst (Executive)
bullets: Generated and managed data for campaign performance reports across 15+ stakeholders, ensuring consistent, high-quality deliverables aligned with client timelines.
Developed real-time campaign performance dashboards in Power BI for data-driven decision-making across political campaign initiatives.
Applied supervised machine learning (Logistic Regression, MLP, Decision Trees) for high-accuracy social media sentiment analysis, achieving 93% accuracy in influencer detection.
Performed data verification, coding checks, and trend analysis using SQL and Excel; produced charts and qualitative insights summarizing key performance drivers.

experience.title: Data Scientist Intern
bullets: Designed a personalized food recommender system using the ALS algorithm and PySpark for multi-cuisine restaurant data.
Developed a custom OCR pipeline with PyTorch and LaTeX for precise mathematical expression recognition from scanned images.
Implemented a facial recognition-based attendance system using OpenCV with real-time detection and 98% accuracy.
Collaborated with senior data scientists on model evaluation, data preprocessing, and production deployment of ML pipelines.

projects.title: Media Intelligence Analytics Platform
projects.description: Built a scalable media intelligence platform processing 10,000+ records/day for real-time brand monitoring, sentiment tracking, and competitor analysis across news, social media, and blogs.
bullets: Engineered ETL pipelines for multi-source content ingestion with automated coding & validation
Built executive dashboards with sentiment distribution, share-of-voice, and alert systems
Delivered actionable insights for 200+ enterprise clients with 99.5% data accuracy

projects.title: Market Research Reporting Automation
projects.description: Designed an end-to-end reporting automation suite for market research teams — automating data extraction, KPI tracking, and report generation across competitor analysis and pricing studies.
bullets: Reduced manual reporting effort by 30% via SQL + Power BI workflow automation
Built real-time KPI dashboards tracking 248 metrics across 15 data sources
Automated 96+ recurring reports/week with 96.2% success rate and alert system

projects.title: Sentiment Analysis & Predictive Analytics
projects.description: Built an end-to-end sentiment analysis pipeline with predictive forecasting — tracking real-time brand mentions across channels and predicting future sentiment trends with 78%+ accuracy.
bullets: Processed 24,800+ mentions across news, social, and blogs with VADER + custom ML models
Built predictive sentiment forecasting achieving 78.6% accuracy on 7-day horizon
Deployed real-time Streamlit dashboard with topic-impact analysis and trend alerts

projects.title: Face Recognition Authentication
projects.description: An AI-powered facial authentication system with real-time live verification, user management, and activity logging. Built with OpenCV, deep learning embeddings, and Flask.
bullets: Real-time webcam detection using OpenCV + DNN face detector with 98.6% accuracy
Built admin dashboard with live authentication, user CRUD, and verification logs
Deployed via Flask REST API achieving <2s response time for 127+ registered users

projects.title: Superstore Sales Analysis Dashboard
projects.description: Interactive Tableau dashboard analyzing 9,994 retail orders across regions, sub-categories, and customer segments — uncovering profit drivers and loss-making areas for executive decision-making.
bullets: Built drill-down visualizations covering $2.3M sales, $286K profit, and 37K units
Analyzed profit/loss patterns across 17 sub-categories and 4 regions (Central, East, South, West)
Highlighted underperforming segments (Tables, Bookcases) and top performers (Phones, Copiers)

projects.title: Personalised Food Recommendation
projects.description: A collaborative-filtering recommendation engine personalizing menu suggestions across multi-cuisine restaurants. Trained on 1,200+ dishes from 43 restaurants and deployed as an interactive Streamlit app.
bullets: Built recommendation engine analyzing 1,238 dishes across 43 restaurants with match scoring
Achieved 39% perfect-match and 74% great+good match rate on user preference profiles
Deployed end-to-end on Streamlit with cuisine, dietary, spice, and budget filters

projects.title: Zomato Ratings Prediction
projects.description: Predictive ML model and EDA on 50,000+ Bengaluru restaurants to identify the key drivers of Zomato ratings. Deployed as a Flask web app so restaurant owners can forecast expected ratings before launch.
bullets: Performed deep EDA on 50,000+ restaurants across 90+ Bengaluru localities
Trained XGBoost regression model achieving R² of 0.85 (RMSE 0.31)
Deployed as a Flask web app with HTML form for instant rating predictions

projects.title: Fake News or Real News Detection
projects.description: NLP-based binary classifier that detects misinformation in online news articles with high precision. Built using TF-IDF vectorization and ensemble ML to combat the spread of fake news.
bullets: Preprocessed and vectorized 40K+ news articles using TF-IDF on stemmed n-grams
Trained ensemble of Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, and Random Forest classifiers
Achieved ~94% accuracy and 0.93 F1-score on held-out test set for fake-vs-real classification

projects.title: AI for Math Equation OCR
projects.description: Custom OCR pipeline that extracts handwritten and printed mathematical equations from images and converts them into LaTeX format. Combines computer vision with sequence-to-sequence deep learning.
bullets: Built end-to-end image-to-LaTeX pipeline using PyTorch encoder-decoder architecture
Trained CNN feature extractor + LSTM-based sequence decoder on 100K+ equation samples
Achieved ~91% BLEU score on equation translation with sub-1-second inference per image

faq.sectionDesc: Common questions about working with me. If yours is not here — just ask.

items.question: What does a freelance data analyst do?
items.answer: I turn raw data into decisions. That means designing SQL/ETL pipelines, building Power BI or Tableau dashboards, automating reporting, and translating findings into recommendations executives can act on. For many clients I also train their internal team so they own the system afterwards.

items.question: How much does a Power BI dashboard cost?
items.answer: A one-off Power BI dashboard build starts at $800 for a simple single-source report and goes up to $2,800 for complex multi-source dashboards with DAX, drill-through and RLS. Retainers start at $1,200/month (20 hrs). Training is $80–$120/hr.

items.question: How long does it take to build an automated reporting dashboard?
items.answer: A basic dashboard is 3–5 days. A production-grade automated system with cleaned pipelines and multiple data sources is typically 1–2 weeks. Every engagement starts with a free 20-min discovery call to scope timeline honestly.

items.question: Do you work with international clients?
items.answer: Yes — I currently serve clients across Europe, North America, and Asia-Pacific. I quote in USD by default, invoice via Wise / Payoneer, and accept SEPA transfers for EU clients. Timezone-wise I overlap comfortably with EU mornings and US afternoons.

items.question: How do you handle data confidentiality?
items.answer: Sample or masked datasets for evaluation, mutual NDA on request, no client data ever stored on personal machines, and delivery via the client's own cloud (SharePoint, Google Drive, S3). All access revoked at project close.

items.question: Can you migrate my Excel reports to Power BI?
items.answer: Yes — Excel → Power BI migration is one of my most-requested engagements. Typical outcome: reports that took 2 days per week now refresh in minutes. Migration usually takes 1 week and costs $1,200–$2,500.

items.question: Do you offer Power BI training?
items.answer: Yes — 1-on-1 and small-team Power BI training covering DAX, data modeling, dashboard design and best practices. Rate: $80–$120/hr (INR ₹2,500/hr for India clients). Full 16-hour course package: ₹36,000.

items.question: What happens after the discovery call?
items.answer: You get a free 20-min call to describe your challenge. Within 24 hours I send back a written scope + fixed-price quote + timeline. If you approve, we start with a 50% deposit; final 50% on delivery. No lock-ins.

caseStudies.sectionIntro: Deep-dives into projects where automation, dashboards and honest scoping moved the numbers. Client identities anonymised on request.

items.title: Patrakaar.AI — AI-Powered Media Intelligence Platform
items.tagline: End-to-end NLP platform for brand & competitor monitoring
items.problem: Enterprises spend ₹10-30 L/month on legacy media-monitoring platforms (Meltwater, CisionOne) that deliver static PDF reports. Analysts still hand-code sentiment, tag topics manually, and copy-paste into Excel. The bottleneck: understanding *why* a story matters, not just that it exists.
solution: Built Patrakaar.AI — an AI agent that ingests news + social feeds, auto-classifies sentiment, extracts entities, and generates executive summaries in Hindi & English
Implemented a RAG pipeline using LangChain + Hugging Face transformers for context-aware Q&A over the daily news corpus
Streamlit dashboard for brand analysts — sentiment trend charts, share-of-voice, competitor benchmarking, and 1-click brief generation
REST API for enterprise integration; Docker-based deployment for on-prem or cloud
Every insight is source-linked — no hallucinations, verifiable citations

items.title: Political Campaign & Election Analytics
items.tagline: Gram-panchayat-level voter analysis for 15+ campaign stakeholders
items.client: Strategy Consulting Firm (Confidential · via ShowTime Consulting)
items.problem: Consulting firm supporting state-level political campaigns needed real-time voter-behavior insights at the gram-panchayat level. Field-team data was fragmented across 20+ Excel files updated daily, making stakeholder briefings slow and inconsistent.
solution: Built ingestion pipeline consolidating daily field-team Excel updates into a unified SQL warehouse
Designed 5+ Power BI dashboards segmented by state, constituency, demographic and issue
Trained influencer-detection models (Logistic Regression, MLP, Decision Trees) — 93% accuracy on labeled test set
Deployed sentiment-analysis Streamlit service for campaign strategists — real-time social listening across regional dialects
Weekly stakeholder briefings automated with executive-ready PPT export

items.title: Sentiment Analysis & Predictive Forecasting
items.client: Personal Project · USA Election Case Study
items.problem: Brand teams get sentiment reports 2-3 weeks late. By then the crisis is already viral. Needed a lightweight pipeline that tracks sentiment across news, Twitter and blogs in near-real-time, plus a 7-day forward projection so PR teams can pre-empt narratives.
solution: Built end-to-end pipeline ingesting 24,800+ mentions across news, social and blog channels
Combined VADER + custom-trained ML models for domain-specific sentiment (political + brand)
Time-series forecasting layer (ARIMA + exponential smoothing) achieving 78.6% directional accuracy on a 7-day horizon
Interactive Streamlit dashboard — topic-impact analysis, alert triggers, and drill-through into the actual mentions
Exportable weekly PDF report auto-generated every Monday morning

items.title: E-commerce Sales Analytics Dashboard
items.problem: Retail executive team was drowning in disconnected sales reports across 4 regions and 17 sub-categories. Wanted a single interactive view to spot profit drivers, loss-making segments, and ship-mode trends — without waiting on the BI team for every question.
solution: Modeled 9,994 retail orders across regions, sub-categories, ship modes and customer segments
Built Tableau dashboard with drill-down from region → state → city → order-level detail
Profit-loss heatmap surfacing underperforming categories (Tables, Bookcases) vs top performers (Phones, Copiers)
Interactive filters for Segment (Consumer / Corporate / Home Office) and Ship Mode with dynamic KPI recalculation
Published to Tableau Public — self-serve executive reporting with zero training required

items.title: Supply Chain Sprint Tracking Dashboard
items.tagline: Live Power BI enhancement + training for SCM Manager
items.client: Supply Chain Manager · SaaS Company (Confidential · India)
items.problem: SCM Manager’s existing Power BI dashboard tracked Sprint / Developer / Stream / Status data but had zero interactivity. Manager had to manually filter and export every time leadership asked “how is Sprint X trending?” — burning 6-8 hours/week.
solution: Added top-level slicers (Sprint / Developer / Stream / Status) with cross-filter behaviour tuned
Built dedicated Drillthrough page for individual Impact IDs — one click, full context
Implemented conditional formatting on Status column with intuitive colour semantics (Green/Amber/Red)
Added Back-button navigation, hidden support pages, and cleaned up model relationships
Delivered foolproof step-by-step documentation so the manager can train new joiners without me
Follow-up: 16-hour Power BI training programme booked (₹36,000)

blog.sectionTitle: Blog · Field Notes from the BI Trenches
blog.sectionIntro: Practical writeups on Power BI patterns, freelance strategy, and analytics case studies — no filler, just what worked.

items.title: 7 Power BI DAX Patterns Every Data Analyst Should Know in 2026
items.excerpt: After 4+ years building Power BI dashboards for 200+ enterprise engagements, these are the 7 DAX patterns I reach for in 90% of my work. Copy-paste ready.
items.author: Gaurav Kumar Vishvakarma
items.content:
After 4+ years building Power BI dashboards for 200+ enterprise engagements, I've noticed something consistent: the dashboards that survive and scale all share a handful of well-written DAX patterns.

The dashboards that get rebuilt every quarter? They reinvent the wheel each time.

So I compiled the 7 DAX patterns I reach for in 90% of my Power BI engagements — patterns that work whether you're tracking BFSI tracker studies, HR attrition, e-commerce funnels, or operational KPIs.

1. The "Date Intelligence" Foundation

Use case: Year-over-year comparisons, month-to-date totals, rolling 12-month averages.

This is the single most reused pattern in enterprise BI. If you can't write Time Intelligence DAX, you can't ship dashboards.

-- Total Sales (base measure)
Total Sales = SUM(Sales[Amount])

-- Sales YTD (Year-to-Date)
Sales YTD = TOTALYTD([Total Sales], 'Date'[Date])

-- Sales PY (Same period prior year)
Sales PY = CALCULATE([Total Sales], SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR('Date'[Date]))

-- YoY % Growth
YoY Growth % = DIVIDE([Total Sales] - [Sales PY], [Sales PY], 0)

The trap to avoid: Don't use EARLIER() for date comparisons. Use a proper date dimension table marked as a date table.

2. The "Top N + Others" Bucket

Use case: Show top 5 products/clients/regions, and bucket the rest as "Others" without filtering them out.

Sales Top 5 + Others =
VAR ProductRank = RANKX(ALLSELECTED(Product[Name]), [Total Sales],, DESC)
VAR Result = IF(ProductRank <= 5, SELECTEDVALUE(Product[Name]), "Others")
RETURN Result

3. Dynamic Measure Selector

Use case: Let users toggle between Sales, Profit, and Units from a single slicer without duplicating visuals.

Create a disconnected table with your measure names, then use SELECTEDVALUE() + SWITCH() to route the calculation.

4. Conditional Formatting via DAX

Use case: Colour cells based on threshold (e.g. red if margin < 10%).

Margin Colour =
VAR M = DIVIDE([Total Profit], [Total Sales])
RETURN SWITCH(TRUE(),
    M < 0.05, "#dc2626",
    M < 0.15, "#f59e0b",
    "#10b981")

5. Cohort Retention

Use case: Track customer retention by acquisition month.

Use USERELATIONSHIP() on an inactive relationship between your Customer[FirstOrderDate] and Date[Date] table to compute cohort buckets cleanly.

6. RLS with USERPRINCIPALNAME()

Use case: Show each regional manager only their region's data — from a single dashboard.

[Region] = LOOKUPVALUE(UserMapping[Region], UserMapping[Email], USERPRINCIPALNAME())

7. Rolling 12-Month Average

Sales R12M =
CALCULATE([Total Sales],
    DATESINPERIOD('Date'[Date], LASTDATE('Date'[Date]), -12, MONTH))

Wrap-up

These 7 patterns cover 80-90% of real dashboard requirements. Nail these and you'll deliver 3x faster than analysts who reinvent every measure from scratch.

Need Power BI training for your team? Book a discovery call — I run 1:1 and small-group programmes.

items.title: How I Landed My First 5 European Freelance Data Analyst Clients (From India)
items.excerpt: The exact playbook: what worked, what didn't, and the templates I used. No fluff — just what a mid-career Indian analyst can copy today.
items.author: Gaurav Kumar Vishvakarma
items.content:
Six months ago I left a comfortable ₹6.3 L job at Acuity Knowledge Partners to go independent. I had a portfolio, some certificates, and a vague idea that "European clients pay in Euros so it must be more."

Today I'm running $1,200-$1,700/month retainers with clients across Germany, the Netherlands and the UK. Here's exactly what worked.

Step 1: Pick a Narrow Positioning (Not "Data Analyst")

Nobody hires a "data analyst" anymore — that's a 500-applicant JD. I positioned as "Independent Power BI Consultant for SMEs migrating from Excel". Instantly:

  • Smaller pond, less competition
  • Clear problem statement clients recognise
  • Justifies premium pricing ($80-$120/hr vs $15/hr generic)

Step 2: Portfolio as Sales Asset (Not a CV)

Your portfolio is not a résumé. It's a sales page. Mine has 6 things:

  1. A one-line promise ("I help businesses replace spreadsheet chaos with automated Power BI dashboards")
  2. Transparent pricing (yes, publicly)
  3. 3-5 case studies with numbers
  4. A "Book Discovery Call" CTA (Calendly + WhatsApp)
  5. FAQs answering the 8 questions every client asks
  6. A downloadable Service Deck PDF

Step 3: Cold Outreach — The 20/day Rule

LinkedIn Sales Navigator, filter by:

  • Company size: 50-500 employees
  • Region: Netherlands, Germany, UK, Nordics
  • Roles: Head of Operations, VP Finance, CFO, COO
  • Industries: E-commerce, SaaS, Fintech, Logistics

Send 20 personalised connect requests per day (never mass-blast). My conversion:

  • 20 requests → 8 accepts
  • 8 accepts → 3 replies
  • 3 replies → 1 discovery call
  • 1 discovery call → 40% become clients

Step 4: Discovery Call Playbook

My call script is dead simple:

  1. 2 min: Their business context
  2. 10 min: The specific pain (what breaks, how often, cost)
  3. 5 min: If I can help, I say "here's roughly what it takes, cost X, timeline Y"
  4. 3 min: Next steps — I email a written scope in 24 hours

No sales language. No pressure. Just: can I help or not?

Step 5: Pricing in USD (Not INR)

Quoting in Rupees signals "cheap Indian freelancer" — even to friendly clients. I quote in USD, invoice via Wise, and receive to my Indian account. The friction cost is under 1%.

Step 6: First 3 Clients — Charge Less, Deliver More

My first Berlin client got a dashboard at 30% below my target rate. Why? For the testimonial + case study rights. That one case study directly landed me the next two clients.

The Numbers, Honestly

Month 10 clients, 200 outreach messages
Month 21 discovery call, no close
Month 31 client, $850 project
Month 42 clients, $2,400 total
Month 53 clients, retainer starts ($1,200/mo)
Month 65 active clients, ~$4,500 MRR

What I'd Do Differently

  • Start the portfolio earlier — I lost 6 weeks tweaking React themes instead of writing case studies
  • Write on LinkedIn from Day 1 — even one post per week compounds
  • Say no faster — I took 2 misfit projects that ate my time and paid poorly

Want the actual cold-outreach templates I use? Ping me on WhatsApp and I'll share the doc.
